codeReview.summary: The master-data-excel feature implementation is complete and functional. It adds Excel import/export to all Master Data modules (COA, Aspek Kerja, Blok) with a reusable ExcelImportModal component featuring file picker, column mapping preview with auto-detect, duplicate Kode detection, validation errors with row-level details, progress bar, and error log download. TypeScript compiles successfully and the implementation follows existing codebase patterns.

issues.description: Auto-detect column mapping uses loose matching (includes) which could incorrectly map columns with similar names. For example, if Excel has columns 'Kode' and 'KodeLama', both would match 'kode' field. Consider using exact matching as primary and contains as fallback.

handleMappingChange function notes that preview would update when user changes mapping, but this is not actually implemented. Changing column mappings in the preview step does not re-parse the data - the user would need to re-select the file.

Tahun Tanam validation upper bound is currentYear + 1. While this is restrictive for planning purposes, it matches the pre-existing BlokFormPage validation. Consider allowing wider range or making it configurable.
When importing Aspek Kerja with coa_kode that doesn't exist in COA, the code silently sets coaId to null without adding a validation error. If users expect the COA linkage, they won't know it failed.
Duplicate warning shows count but doesn't show which Kode values are duplicates. Consider listing the duplicate Kode values so users know what will be skipped.
Import path is '../electron.d.ts' instead of '../types/electron.d.ts'. While TypeScript declaration files work when referenced in tsconfig, using consistent paths is better practice.
When dialog:showSave is called with mainWindow!, if mainWindow is null (which shouldn't happen in normal flow), this would throw. Consider adding a null check for defensive programming.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation follows existing codebase patterns for IPC handlers and React components. No significant deviations from established conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All IPC handlers follow the same pattern as existing handlers (log.debug, return value). React components use functional patterns consistent with existing code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the frontend-worker skill procedure correctly. No deviations noted in skillFeedback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows followedProcedure: true with no deviations. The worker created a reusable component and integrated it properly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new knowledge gaps discovered. The xlsx library was already in use for export, and the same patterns were extended for import.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Excel processing was already implemented for export in all modules. The new import feature extended these patterns.

summary: The master-data-excel feature is well-implemented with all core requirements met: file picker for .xlsx, column mapping preview with auto-detect, duplicate detection, validation errors, progress bar, and error log download. The reusable ExcelImportModal component is a good architectural decision. No blocking issues found. TypeScript compiles successfully. The issues noted are non-blocking suggestions for improvement.
